g.text: Read CR spec, status OCID, and delete intent
Keep status-bound OCID authoritative for later update or delete paths
Get the OCI resource by known identifier
Resolve an existing OCI resource by display name
Continue list pagination until a match or exhaustion
Persist the resolved or created OCID back into status
- Pagination and field-level drift are still documented
primarily in Go code and property tests.
- The services collection is reconciled in implementation,
but the current formal profile does not model it as a
Submit OCI delete for OCI Service Gateway
Confirm deletion with follow-up GetServiceGateway calls until the resource is gone or not found.
Remove the finalizer after OCI deletion is confirmed
Request requeue and keep the finalizer
Return an unsuccessful terminal reconcile result
Compare live OCI state with the supported drift surface
Reject the change before any OCI mutation
Continue full pagination until a display-name match is found or all pages are exhausted.
Reuse the resolved OCID for all later update and delete operations once the gateway is found.
Move the Service Gateway compartment before calling the mutable update path when compartment drift exists.
Compare the services collection order-insensitively before deciding whether UpdateServiceGateway is required.
Apply only the supported in-place update surface
Return success for the usable active state
